rules: All functions must have a single responsibility (SRP)
Use dependency injection instead of hardcoding dependencies
All public methods must have input validation
No magic numbers or hardcoded strings — use named constants
Error handling must be explicit — no silent catches or bare except/catch
Functions should not exceed 30 lines of code
Maximum 3 levels of nesting (if/for/while) per function
Use meaningful variable and function names — no single-letter names except loop indices
All async operations must have proper error handling

JavaScript: Use const by default, let only when reassignment is needed, never var
Use async/await over raw Promises and callbacks
Use strict equality (===) instead of loose equality (==)
Use template literals instead of string concatenation
Destructure objects and arrays when accessing multiple properties
TypeScript: All functions must have explicit return types
Use interfaces for object shapes, types for unions/intersections
No use of 'any' type — use 'unknown' if the type is uncertain
Use enums or const objects for fixed sets of values
Python: Use type hints for all function parameters and return types
Use dataclasses or Pydantic models for data structures
Use context managers (with statements) for resource management
Use list comprehensions over map/filter where readable
